Publisher of February Financial Plan - 2022 Adopted Budget Total Positions by Function and Agency
Public transportation authority serving the New York metropolitan area
North America's largest transportation network, serving 15.3 million people across 5,000-square-mile area.
Publisher and parent agency for LIRR/MNR PTC Committee Meeting
Publisher of the Bicycle, Pedestrian and Micromobility Strategic Action Plan
Subject of the NYPD MTA Crime Report

The MTA provides transportation services in the New York metropolitan area, operations of seven bridges and two tunnels within New York City and primary insurance coverage to the MTA related entities.
Preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region it serves through the cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable, and clean transportation services.
MTA's fuel hedging program manages Ultra-Low Sulfur Diesel (ULSD) hedges

records.display_name: E 167th St / E 168th St Bus Priority Improvements

payload.description: Proposed bus priority improvements on E 167th St/E 168th St corridor including queue jump signals, segments of bus/bike lanes, curb management, and proposed curbside bus lane from Jerome Ave
payload.project_name: E 167th St / E 168th St Bus Priority Improvements

records.raw_text: E 167th St / E168th St. Proposed Treatments: Queue Jump Signals, Segments of Bus/Bike Lanes, Curb Management. Proposed curbside bus lane from Jerome Ave. Proposed new meters and truck loading area.

records.display_name: E.L. Grant Highway Bus Priority and Safety Improvements

payload.description: Proposed bus priority and safety improvements on E.L. Grant Highway including offset bus lanes, center running bus lane, bus boarding islands, protected bike lanes, pedestrian safety improvements, and intersection redesign at 170 St/Shakespeare Ave/Nelson Ave
payload.project_name: E.L. Grant Highway Bus Priority and Safety Improvements

records.raw_text: Bx35 Bus Priority and Safety Improvements. Proposed Treatment: offset bus lanes, center running bus lane, bus boarding islands, physical protection. Benefits: Improves bus speeds and reliability, Improves bus stop accessibility, Shortens crossing distances, Enhances pedestrian safety, Improves bicycle safety, Reduces double parking or barriers to bus lane

records.display_name: Connecting Communities: Harlem River Bridge Access

payload.description: Edward L Grant Highway identified as priority project in the Connecting Communities Plan (May 2018). Protected bicycle lane improves bicycle and pedestrian safety and enhances access to/from Manhattan via Washington Bridge. Create pedestrian and bicycle gateway to bridge as part of planned NYSDOT capital project. Upgrade existing bicycle route on E L Grant Hwy to protected path from E 167 St to Tremont Ave.
payload.project_name: Connecting Communities: Harlem River Bridge Access

records.raw_text: Edward L Grant Highway identified as priority project in the Connecting Communities Plan (May 2018). Protected bicycle lane improves bicycle and pedestrian safety and enhances access to/from Manhattan via Washington Bridge. Over 350 cyclists and 515 pedestrians use the Washington Bridge daily (7am-7pm). 3B Edward L Grant Highway / University Ave Create pedestrian and bicycle gateway to bridge as part of planned NYSDOT capital project. Upgrade existing bicycle route on E L Grant Hwy to protected path from E 167 St to Tremont Ave.
